messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0010",
  "title": "少数巨头的生态守卫战 vs 庞大长尾的残酷生存战",
  "page_index": 5,
  "page_range": "5"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0011",
    "title": "2023年",
    "page_range": "5",
    "summary": "This section answers questions regarding the 2023 mobile game market revenue distribution by ranking tier. It provides quantitative metrics (revenue scale proportions) for four entity categories: top 1–10 ranked games (43.14%), 11–50 ranked games (26.62%), 51–200 ranked games (19.45%), and other mobile games (10.79%). The content serves as statistical evidence for market concentration analysis in 2023. It does not contain definitions, procedures, qualitative claims, tables, or figures."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0012",
    "title": "2024年",
    "page_range": "5",
    "summary": "This section provides 2024 quantitative data on mobile game revenue distribution by ranking tier. It answers retrieval queries regarding:\n- **Date:** 2024\n- **Topic:** Mobile game revenue concentration and market share distribution\n- **Entities/Categories:** Ranking tiers (1–10, 11–50, 51–200) and \"other\" mobile games\n- **Metrics & Values:** Revenue scale proportions: 43.23% (1–10), 28.68% (11–50), 18.62% (51–200), and 9.47% (others)\n- **Evidence Type:** Statistical market share breakdown\nSuitable for questions about revenue concentration, tier-based distribution, or specific percentage shares for mobile games in 2024. No definitions, procedures, claims, tables, or figures are present."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0013",
    "title": "2025年",
    "page_range": "5-1",
    "summary": "This section enables retrieval of answers to questions regarding:\n- **Metrics & Dates:** 2025 mobile game revenue share by ranking tier (1-10: 44.01%; 11-50: 27.20%; 51-200: 19.08%; Others: 9.71%).\n- **Figures:** A color-coded bar chart visualizing these shares (blue: 1-10, cyan: 11-50, green: 51-200, orange: others) with representative game icons displayed above.\n- **Claims & Trends:** Three-year market concentration patterns, specifically that the top-10 tier fluctuates slightly but remains near 43%, mid-tier brackets (11-50 and 51-200) show noticeable variation, and the \"other\" category remains stable and low.\n- **Topics & Entities:** Mobile game market revenue distribution, ranking-based market share brackets, 2025 industry performance data.\n- **Evidence Types:** Independent research data and proprietary chart visualization sourced from Diandian Data (点点数据).\n- **Definitions & Procedures:** Not addressed in this section."
  }
]

message.content: This section examines mobile game market revenue distribution and concentration across ranking tiers from 2023 to 2025, illustrating the competitive contrast between top-tier dominance and long-tail survival. It provides quantitative metrics for four entity categories (ranked 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, and others) across three dates: 2023 (43.14%, 26.62%, 19.45%, 10.79%), 2024 (43.23%, 28.68%, 18.62%, 9.47%), and 2025 (44.01%, 27.20%, 19.08%, 9.71%). The content includes a color-coded bar chart with representative game icons visualizing the 2025 data. It supports claims regarding three-year market concentration trends, specifically that the top-10 tier fluctuates slightly but remains near 43%, mid-tier brackets show noticeable variation, and the "other" category remains stable and low. Evidence types consist of statistical market share breakdowns and independent research data with proprietary chart visualizations sourced from Diandian Data (点点数据). The section does not contain definitions, procedures, or tables.

response_text: This section examines mobile game market revenue distribution and concentration across ranking tiers from 2023 to 2025, illustrating the competitive contrast between top-tier dominance and long-tail survival. It provides quantitative metrics for four entity categories (ranked 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, and others) across three dates: 2023 (43.14%, 26.62%, 19.45%, 10.79%), 2024 (43.23%, 28.68%, 18.62%, 9.47%), and 2025 (44.01%, 27.20%, 19.08%, 9.71%). The content includes a color-coded bar chart with representative game icons visualizing the 2025 data. It supports claims regarding three-year market concentration trends, specifically that the top-10 tier fluctuates slightly but remains near 43%, mid-tier brackets show noticeable variation, and the "other" category remains stable and low. Evidence types consist of statistical market share breakdowns and independent research data with proprietary chart visualizations sourced from Diandian Data (点点数据). The section does not contain definitions, procedures, or tables.
